subject: Re: M refinement of subtomograms lowers resolution
body:
Hi Alister,

Thanks! That's exactly what I did! Thanks so much for writing these
scripts! :)
What puzzles me is that it worked for the binned particles, but not for the
refined unbinned ones. Not sure what went wrong the second time.

Best,
Fabian



On Wed, 20 Jan 2021 at 17:25, Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi Fabian,
>
> Which version of RELION are you using for refinement?
>
> If you’re using RELION 3.1+ the shifts stored in the star file are in
> angstroms rather than pixels. If you have a RELION 3.1 star file to Warp
> and just removed the optics table then your shifts are not being included
> in the input into M.
>
> If you think this might be your problem, I have a little script which you
> can run post-refinement but before particle extraction here:
> https://github.com/alisterburt/dynamo2m
>
> The script is ‘relion_star_downgrade’, you can run it directly from the
> command line after running ‘pip install dynamo2m’.
>
> Hope that’s useful,
>
> Alister
>
> On 20 Jan 2021, at 07:37, Fabian Eisenstein <eisenstein@m.u-tokyo.ac.jp>
> wrote:
>
> ﻿Hey all!
>
> I am doing some subtomogram averaging of boring purified ribosomes (FISE
> TS acquisition).
> I imported motion corrected tilt images to Warp, exported subtomograms in
> bin2, refined and classified in relion, imported the result into M and
> refined which left me at a resolution of about 7.4 Å. I exported  the
> unbinned subtomograms (1.4 Å/px) and did another round of refinement in
> relion resulting in 6.8 Å.
>
> When I now refine in M, it always ends up at resolutions of 25-30 Å. I
> also ran it with refinement of defocus or particle poses only.
> Any idea where I could have messed up the input?
>
> Thanks a lot and best,
> Fabian
>
> --
